summary: Backend fix for super_admin bootstrap/recovery logic in an admin management system using Principal-based authentication. The system has a role model with 'super_admin' and 'admin' as mutually exclusive single-field roles.
architectural_notes: Role model: single role field with mutually exclusive values 'super_admin' or 'admin'. No flags or multi-role arrays.
Bootstrap logic: if Admin table is empty, first logged-in Principal must be assigned role = super_admin.
Recovery logic: inside authenticated update calls requiring admin privileges, if super_admin_count == 0, automatically promote caller to super_admin (no separate recovery function).
Role promotion is replacement (overwrite), not additive.
known_issues: Bootstrap logic did not assign super_admin correctly — all existing users have role 'admin', no super_admin exists, system locked. Fix: recovery logic to auto-promote caller when super_admin_count == 0.

new_feature_requests.summary: Fix the bootstrap logic in the backend so that when the Admin table is empty and a Principal logs in for the first time, that Principal is assigned the role 'super_admin' instead of 'admin'.

Add automatic recovery logic inside authenticated update calls that require admin privileges: if super_admin_count == 0, automatically promote the calling Principal's role to 'super_admin' (replace existing 'admin' role). This must not be a separate dedicated function — it must run inline within existing privileged update calls.

Update the migration module (backend/migration.mo) to ensure that when migrating legacy admin records, if no record carries role 'super_admin' after migration, the first migrated record is promoted to 'super_admin' rather than leaving all records as 'admin'.
